Fix neo4j container startup failure caused by read-only volume mount

Neo4j 5 chowns everything under /var/lib/neo4j/ on startup, which
fails on the :ro mounted init-graph.cypher. The file is already
mounted separately in the neo4j-init sidecar container.

Spring Boot 3.4 / Java 21 app with Nginx load balancer, Post Service
(PostgreSQL + Redis cache), Fanout Service (RabbitMQ → workers → News Feed
Cache), Neo4j graph DB for friendships, Prometheus/Grafana monitoring,
load test client, unit tests, and Docker Compose orchestration.

commit.message:
feat(logging): add structured JSON log handler alongside text log (#2)

* feat(logging): add structured JSON log handler alongside text log

Summary:
- Add logger_json ~> 7.0 dependency to mix.exs
- Add secondary rotating JSON handler to LogFile using
  LoggerJSON.Formatters.Basic with metadata: :all
- Add default_json_log_file/0,1 path helpers for log/symphony.jsonl
- Teach set_logs_root/1 to relocate JSON log alongside text log
- Expose config flags: :json_log_file, :json_log_file_max_bytes,
  :json_log_file_max_files, :json_log_enabled
- JSON handler fails gracefully with warning if it cannot attach
- Refactor remove_existing_handler into generic remove_handler/1
- Add tests for JSON log path helpers and set_logs_root/1 coverage

Rationale:
- Text log is great for humans but not queryable or shippable to
  log aggregators. A parallel JSONL stream enables jq filtering,
  Promtail/Alloy scraping, and future Loki integration with zero
  format transformation.
- Failure isolation ensures the JSON handler never blocks boot.

Tests:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
- mix test (252 tests, 0 failures in changed code; 1 pre-existing
  flaky timing test in OrchestratorStatusTest)
- mix credo --strict on changed file: no issues

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* WORKFLOW git

* removing text logging - going with JSON for now on

* pr description lint my balls

* just spec stuff

* mostly credo issues fixes

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify XDigitAllBitsUnknown → §5.7.1 [skip ci]

§5.7.1 defines the grammar for decimal_number, including the production `[ size ] decimal_base x_digit { _ }` which covers `8'dx`. The LRM explicitly states that a decimal literal may contain a single x or z digit to indicate all bits are unknown/high-impedance. No deeper subclause exists for this construct.

* fix(format): isort import order in app/main.py (macro_router after etf routers)

* refactor: systematic code optimize + cleanup pass (Phases 1–7)

## Backend

### Route architecture (app/routes/ → feature modules)
- Migrate 25 legacy routes to app/features/*/routes/ modules
- Only model.py (714L) and portfolio_management.py (982L) remain in
  app/routes/ pending service extraction
- Delete dead code: app/routes/ensemble.py (never registered),
  app/routes/signals.py (superseded), tests/test_ensemble_api.py
- Update app/main.py: extract 8 _startup_*()/_shutdown_*() functions;
  lifespan body reduced to ~10 lines; fix docstring

### Shared job utilities
- jobs/job_utils.py: get_db_connection(), record_job_completion(),
  persist_shap_to_db() (de-duplicated from 3 signal job scripts)
- jobs/price_utils.py: get_universe_symbols(), to_yfinance_ticker(),
  to_internal_symbol(), upsert_prices_batch()

### Repository & service decomposition
- portfolio_repository.py 1130L→941L via mixin inheritance
  (PortfolioPriceRepository + PortfolioSignalRepository)
- health_score_service.py 852L→576L; pure functions extracted to
  health_score_calculator.py (448L)
- feature_engine.py 990L→868L; feature groups split into
  feature_groups/momentum.py, volatility.py, fundamental.py

### asyncpg migration
- async_signal_repository.py: 6 new async methods
- signal_repository.py: all 9 sync methods marked DEPRECATED
- signal_service.py: fully migrated to AsyncSignalRepository
- health_score_service._gather_financial_data(): migrated to asyncpg

### Bug fixes (found by new tests)
- log_webhook_routes.py: move import json/os to module level (was
  inside try block causing UnboundLocalError on 401 paths)
- log_webhook_routes.py: add except HTTPException: raise before
  generic except to prevent 401s being re-wrapped as 500s

## Frontend

### lib/api.ts domain split (687L → 57L true barrel)
- lib/api/models-api.ts: model status, drift, feature importance,
  portfolio attribution/performance, model compare
- lib/api/signals-api.ts: live signals, ensemble, Model B,
  fundamentals, ASX announcements
- lib/api/health-score-api.ts: health score types + functions
- lib/api/expense-api.ts: expense insights + loan summary
- lib/api.ts: true export * barrel + getHealth/sendAssistantChat inline

### lib/api-client.ts
- Remove 8 dead methods
- Add AbortSignal forwarding to all 28 remaining methods

### Component decomposition
- AssistantClient.tsx 606L→290L (components/assistant/ subdir)
- ModelsClient.tsx 566L→185L (ModelTabNav + ModelATab extracted)
- NotificationSettings.tsx 477L→264L (2 sections extracted)
- signals-api.ts 408L→50L barrel (4 domain API files)

### Performance (memoization)
- FusionDashboard.tsx: 8 useMemo/useCallback sites; Intl formatters
  moved to module scope
- DriftMonitoringDashboard.tsx: 10 useMemo/useCallback sites; fix
  state mutation bug (sort() on state array)
- HoldingsTable.tsx: totalPLPercent added to useMemo; useCallback
  for handleGlobalFilterChange

### Contracts
- signal.contract.ts renamed to ml-signals.contract.ts
- jest.config.js: exclude lib/api/*.ts domain slices from coverage

### Tests
- tests/test_log_webhook_routes.py: 13 tests covering signature
  verification paths for Vercel, Render, Supabase webhooks
- Update patch paths for migrated routes in 7 existing test files

Net: ~52 files changed, +1700 / -11521 lines

commit.message:
Add async WAL writer with group commit for vertical scaling

Replace the global WAL Mutex with a WalHandle abstraction that supports
two modes: Inline (synchronous Mutex, used for tests and CLI) and Channel
(async mpsc + dedicated writer task, used in server mode).

In server mode, concurrent writers send ops through an mpsc channel to a
background WAL task. The task drains all queued messages, batches them,
and does a single fsync for the entire batch — the classic "group commit"
pattern used by Postgres and SQLite. This eliminates the global write
mutex bottleneck and lets write throughput scale with core count.

Also adds concurrent read/write benchmarks to engine.rs and a Concurrency
section to the README with throughput scaling numbers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify DecimalSizedBaseElaborates → §5.7.1 [skip ci]

The test assigns a sized decimal literal `8'd200` to a logic variable and checks that elaboration succeeds. §5.7.1 defines integer literal constants, including the sized based literal form (size + apostrophe + base + digits), which is exactly what `8'd200` is.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: OpenAI Realtime API voice pipeline (replaces STT→Claude→TTS chain)

Adds a bidirectional WebSocket bridge between Twilio Media Streams and the
OpenAI Realtime API (gpt-4o-realtime-preview), cutting per-turn latency from
4-9 s down to ~0.5-1.5 s.

Gate: REALTIME_ENABLED=true on Render activates the new flow.
      REALTIME_ENABLED=false (default) leaves the existing /twilio/turn
      HTTP flow completely untouched — instant rollback available.

New file: app/routes/realtime.py
  - WebSocket /twilio/media-stream bridges Twilio <-> OpenAI Realtime
  - G.711 µ-law 8kHz audio flows natively — zero codec conversion
  - Server-side VAD (800 ms silence threshold, tunable via env var)
  - Barge-in: speech_started → response.cancel + Twilio clear event,
    with drain flag to suppress buffered audio deltas after interruption
  - All TOOL_EXECUTORS reused unchanged (Acuity, SMS, Sheets, etc.)
  - escalate_to_claude tool delegates complex reasoning to Claude Sonnet
    with logging for every invocation (for prompt improvement over time)
  - Transfer via Twilio REST API calls(sid).update() mid-stream
  - OpenAI WebSocket reconnect on unexpected drop (one attempt, 5 s timeout)
  - Session load + clinic init completes BEFORE greeting fires
  - Session saved to Redis on every tool call and on disconnect

Modified: app/routes/twilio.py
  - /twilio/voice: REALTIME_ENABLED branch returns <Connect><Stream> TwiML
  - All other routes (/turn, /status, /transfer-status) unchanged

Modified: app/config.py
  - REALTIME_ENABLED, REALTIME_VOICE, REALTIME_VAD_SILENCE_MS, OPENAI_API_KEY

Modified: requirements.txt
  - openai bumped to >=1.50.0 (Realtime API support)
  - websockets>=13.0 pinned explicitly

Env vars to add on Render:
  REALTIME_ENABLED=true
  REALTIME_VOICE=coral        (British female; shimmer is alternative)
  REALTIME_VAD_SILENCE_MS=800 (optional, this is the default)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add check-network.rb and extend check-proxy.rb for office network diagnostics

  - Add --office mode to check all three office proxies at once
  - Refactor curl call into curl_check helper

commit.message:
Fix vector search result ID mismatch breaking RRF hybrid search

Vector search results used Qdrant point IDs (UUID v5) instead of
original report IDs, causing RRF fusion to treat same reports as
different documents and silently dropping all vector search results.

Now uses payload.report_id to match BM25 report IDs.

commit.message:
fix: stabilize player modal height on iOS Safari

Replace 100dvh with inherited fixed inset:0 for the player modal overlay
on mobile. 100dvh changes dynamically as Safari's address bar shows/hides,
causing the modal to shift height during scrolling. Using the stable
position:fixed inset:0 from the desktop rule and 100%-based max-height
ensures consistent positioning.

commit.message:
feat: add Batch 4 — PasswordInput, RadioGroup, Switch, FormGroup, FormActions, FormError

6 new agnostic form primitives extracted from DearAdry admin patterns:
- PasswordInput: input with show/hide toggle, paste prevention
- RadioGroup + Radio: radio button group with vertical/horizontal direction
- Switch: toggle switch with label (differs from Toggle in semantics)
- FormGroup: vertical spacing wrapper for form fields
- FormActions: aligned button container (left/right/center)
- FormError: form-level and field-level error messages

All include Storybook stories with autodocs.
